Client 2: And I’m Mary—peace of mind, really. I don’t want this to feel complicated every month.

Advisor: Hi Thomas, hi Mary—good to meet you both. I’m glad you came in. Before we get into the weeds, what prompted you to set this up now?
Client 1: Thanks. I’m Thomas. We’re retired, and honestly we just want to make sure we’re not making a dumb withdrawal or tax mistake.
Client 2: And I’m Mary—peace of mind, really. I don’t want this to feel complicated every month.
Advisor: That makes sense. Let’s start high level with cash flow, then we’ll map the accounts and any insurance. Thomas, roughly what’s your household gross income per year right now—before taxes?

Advisor: Florida, okay. And country-wise, just confirming—are we talking U.S. for residency and taxes?
Client 1: Yes, country is US.
Advisor: Great. And then, Thomas, Mary—how did you come to us? Are you looking for ongoing investment management and planning through an RIA, or more of a one-time plan?
Client 1: We’re looking for the RIA (registered investment advisor) approach—ongoing, not just a one-time plan.
Advisor: Okay, that helps. Mary, when you say “peace of mind,” is it mostly that you want a steady monthly picture—like income, spending, and what to pull from which account?

Client 1: Thanks. I’m Thomas. We’re retired, and honestly we just want to make sure we’re not making a dumb withdrawal or tax mistake.

Advisor: Got it—thank you. And Mary, on the spending side, what do your total monthly expenses usually run, all-in?

Advisor: Deal. I’ll keep it straightforward.
Advisor: Alright—before we stop for today, I want to make sure I’ve got a couple household basics right, so I don’t assume anything.
Advisor: Thomas, Mary—marital status: are you two married?
Client 1: Yes, we’re married.
Advisor: Great. And just confirming household size—how many adults are in the household? Is it just the two of you?

Client 2: I always mix those up—sorry. Is that the percentage one?
Advisor: Yep, exactly—the percentage bracket you’re generally in.
Client 1: We plan around 35%.
Advisor: Okay, thank you. Let me note that down.
Advisor: And just so I’m tying this together: married, two adults, moderate risk, and you’re planning around that 35% bracket—does that sound right?

Advisor: Great. And just confirming household size—how many adults are in the household? Is it just the two of you?
Client 2: Yes, it’s 2 adults—just Thomas and me.

Client 1: I’d call it moderate.
Client 2: Yeah—moderate. We don’t want anything too wild.
